  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "NM cable is routed through bored holes in wood studs less than 1-1/4 inches from the nearest edge of the stud without protection by a steel plate."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a steel nail plate (minimum 1/16 in. thick) on the face of the stud where the NM cable passes through to protect it from future drywall screws or nails."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 300.4(A)(1) requires that where NM cable is run through bored holes in joists, rafters, or wood members, the holes must be bored so that the edge of the hole is not less than 1-1/4 inches from the nearest edge of the wood member. If this distance cannot be maintained, the cable must be protected from penetration by screws or nails by a steel plate at least 1/16 inches thick.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 300.4(A)(1)",
    "NEC 334"
  ]
}
